title: G5: Hypothesis test, decision, and honest power/exclusion statement

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Gap.** The problem asks for a decision: are the perpendicular components consistent with zero, or significantly nonzero? This requires a stated statistical test, not a qualitative remark.

**What to do.** (i) Null $H_0$: $v_{\perp,i}=0\ \forall i$. (ii) Per-satellite significance $z_i = v_{\perp,i}/\sigma_i$. (iii) Ensemble statistic using the FULL covariance from G4: $\chi^2 = \mathbf v_\perp^{\!\top}\,\Sigma_{v_\perp}^{-1}\,\mathbf v_\perp$, compared to $\chi^2_k$ with $k$ = number of satellites with PMs; report the $p$-value. (iv) POWER ANALYSIS: inject synthetic $v_\perp$ of amplitude $A$, recompute the test, and report the $A$ detectable at $3\sigma$ given current $\sigma_i$.

**Why critical.** As of ~2018 only a handful of on-plane satellites have measured PMs and per-mas/century errors map to $\sim37$ km/s. The test may have limited power. The solution must state this *partial solvability* quantitatively (what can and cannot be concluded) rather than overclaiming. Using a diagonal covariance (ignoring G2/G3 correlations) would falsely inflate significance.
